message:
chore: remove accidentally tracked fuzz/target/ build artifacts

Delete 360 files under fuzz/target/ that were previously committed to
the repository. These are cargo build artifacts (compiled libraries,
fingerprints, incremental compilation cache, object files, .rmeta,
.rlib, .d dependency files, build script outputs) generated by
cargo-fuzz during fuzzing runs.

Build artifacts should not be tracked in version control — they are
machine-specific, non-reproducible, and bloat the repository. The
fuzz/target/ directory should be covered by .gitignore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
